2603.23091.title: When Language Models Lose Their Mind: The Consequences of Brain Misalignment
2603.23091.paper_summary: While brain-aligned large language models (LLMs) have garnered attention for their potential as cognitive models and for potential for enhanced safety and trustworthiness in AI, the role of this brain alignment for linguistic competence remains uncertain. In this work, we investigate the functional implications of brain alignment by introducing brain-misaligned models--LLMs intentionally trained to predict brain activity poorly while maintaining high language modeling performance. We evaluate these models on over 200 downstream tasks encompassing diverse linguistic domains, including semantics, syntax, discourse, reasoning, and morphology. By comparing brain-misaligned models with well-matched brain-aligned counterparts, we isolate the specific impact of brain alignment on language understanding. Our experiments reveal that brain misalignment substantially impairs downstream performance, highlighting the critical role of brain alignment in achieving robust linguistic competence. These findings underscore the importance of brain alignment in LLMs and offer novel insights into the relationship between neural representations and linguistic processing.
2603.23091.paper_summary_zh: 雖然與大腦對齊的大型語言模型（LLMs）因其作為認知模型的潛力以及在人工智慧中的安全性和可信度的增強潛力而受到關注，但這種大腦對齊對語言能力的作用仍然不確定。在本研究中，我們通過引入大腦錯位模型來探討大腦對齊的功能意涵——這些LLMs故意訓練以不良地預測大腦活動，同時保持高語言建模性能。我們在超過200個下游任務上評估這些模型，涵蓋多樣的語言領域，包括語義、語法、話語、推理和形態學。通過將大腦錯位模型與良好匹配的大腦對齊模型進行比較，我們隔離了大腦對齊對語言理解的具體影響。我們的實驗顯示，大腦錯位顯著損害下游性能，突顯了大腦對齊在實現穩健語言能力中的關鍵作用。這些發現強調了大腦對齊在LLMs中的重要性，並提供了對神經表徵與語言處理之間關係的新見解。
2603.23091.author: Gabriele Merlin et.al.
